howItWorks.intro: This strait of hormuz gas price impact calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Price multiplier = 1 + (global supply loss × price elasticity factor)
Monthly fuel cost = (monthly miles / MPG) × gas price
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz?
faq.answer: Approximately 17 million barrels per day, representing about 21% of global oil consumption and roughly one-third of all seaborne oil trade.

faq.question: Has the Strait of Hormuz ever been closed?
faq.answer: It has never been fully closed, but Iran has threatened closure multiple times. During the Iran-Iraq War (1980-88), both nations attacked tankers in the strait. In 2019, Iran seized a British-flagged tanker, briefly disrupting traffic.

faq.question: How quickly would gas prices rise if the strait closed?
faq.answer: Oil futures would spike within hours. Pump prices typically follow within 1-3 weeks. During past Middle East crises, gas prices have risen 30-80% within weeks.

faq.question: Could the US military keep the strait open?
faq.answer: The US 5th Fleet is based in Bahrain specifically to protect strait shipping. However, Iran has extensive anti-ship missile capabilities, mines, and fast-attack boats that could disrupt traffic even against military opposition.

faq.question: Is the Strait of Hormuz Gas Price Impact Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Strait of Hormuz Gas Price Impact Calculator?
sections.body: The Strait of Hormuz Gas Price Impact Calculator is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your current gas price, disruption level, monthly miles driven, you get instant results including projected gas price, price increase, current monthly fuel cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting projected gas price right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current gas price and need to find the right projected gas price. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Gas Price ($/gal)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current gas price — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: disruption level, monthly miles driven, vehicle mpg. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your projected gas price, price increase, current monthly fuel cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current gas price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both projected gas price and price increase — they work together to give you the full picture.
